mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the effective federal funds rate over the prior three months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present value and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, independent of regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or the sign of the correlation flips in more than one of four consecutive 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals an accelerating labor-market deterioration that predicts earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger fires, mean forward 60d SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) relative to unconditional mean, across at least two distinct historical episodes visible pre-2016-01-15.

mechanism_narrative: The joint state of low FEDFUNDS combined with elevated UNRATE indicates a policy accommodation stance calibrated to labor slack; when both conditions co-occur, forward-looking equity risk premia compress more than either variable predicts alone, generating positive forward 20-day S&P 500 returns beyond the additive effect of each series.
failure_condition: The interaction term's incremental Rank IC over the sum of single-series IC contributions is not statistically distinguishable from zero (bootstrap p > 0.10) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns during expansionary regimes, but the same rise signals overheating and prompts risk-off repricing during late-cycle or recession-recovery regimes, flipping the return sign conditional on the macro regime.
failure_condition: The direction of forward 60d returns conditional on the RSAFS growth trigger does not differ significantly (chi-square test p > 0.10) between GDPC1-classified recovery and late-cycle subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization, which is rapidly priced into equities within one week, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly INDPRO change and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating the signal decays before the horizon.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below a 12-month moving average by more than 10%, it signals a contraction in interest-rate-sensitive demand that precedes broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% below trailing 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Mean forward 60d SPX return following triggered instances is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.10) across at least two distinct historical cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, the implied deeply negative real policy rate signals sustained monetary accommodation that fuels equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; this effect requires the joint condition of rising inflation and near-zero nominal rates rather than either series alone.
regime_hint: zero-lower-bound monetary accommodation with rising inflation
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns during triggered joint-condition periods are not significantly different from returns during periods when only one of the two conditions holds (ANOVA p > 0.10).
